laws.title: Public Law 11-71 | Supreme Court Needs Act
laws.status_basis: Section 2(a) repeals 28 U.S.C. section 1 and section 2(b) immediately replaces it with new text setting the Court at a Chief Justice plus four associate justices. The source contains no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the Supreme Court Needs Act.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 28 U.S.C. section 1 and replaces it with new Supreme Court membership and quorum text.
provisions.exact_change: Replace 28 U.S.C. section 1 with text stating that the Supreme Court consists of a Chief Justice and four associate justices and may determine quorum.
provisions.evidence: 28 U.S. Code section 1 is repealed. 28 U.S. Code section 1 shall read as: The Supreme Court of the United States shall consist of a Chief Justice of the United States and four associate justices.
provisions.notes: This is a determinable strike-and-replace amendment, not a note.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect upon passage.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note sits at the correct anchor but only adds boilerplate with a full-text dump and Trello link; 28 U.S.C. section 1 was not actually amended.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl011071-codification.
Actually amend 28 U.S.C. section 1 to the new Chief Justice plus four associate justices text and add a proper amendment history note.

laws.title: Public Law 11-72 | No More Mariah Act
laws.status_basis: Section 2 repeals Public Law 10-65 and section 3 repeals and replaces 28 U.S.C. section 133 with a new District of Columbia judgeship rule. The source itself does not self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the No More Mariah Act.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 10-65, the Federal Judicial Reform Act II.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal of prior public law PL 10-65.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 10-65, the Federal Judicial Reform Act II is repealed.
provisions.notes: Record as a repeal cross-reference; it is not Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals and re-enacts 28 U.S.C. section 133 for District of Columbia district judges.
provisions.exact_change: Replace 28 U.S.C. section 133 with the two-subsection rule allowing up to five District of Columbia judges and a replacement mechanism for inactive or unfit judges.
provisions.evidence: 28 U.S. Code section 133 - Appointment and number of district judges is repealed. 28 U.S. Code section 133 - Appointment and number of district judges shall read as:
provisions.notes: This requires actual textual amendment of section 133.

provisions.text_summary: Contingent effective date tied to the Federal Circuit Court of the District of Columbia being vacated.
provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect when the Federal Circuit Court of the District of Columbia is entirely vacated.
provisions.notes: Treat as a contingent effective-date clause.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is anchored at section 1, but the real target is 28 U.S.C. section 133. The draft only added boilerplate with a full-text dump and Trello link and did not record the repeal of Public Law 10-65.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl011072-codification.
Amend 28 U.S.C. section 133 with the new District of Columbia judgeship text and add a contingent effective-date note.
Record the repeal of Public Law 10-65 as a repeal cross-reference.

laws.title: Public Law 11-73 | Let Me Act
laws.status_basis: Section 2 creates a generally applicable acting-service rule for nominees waiting for Senate confirmation. The source contains no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the Let Me Act.

provisions.text_summary: Allows nominees to act in office while awaiting Senate confirmation for seven listed office categories.
provisions.exact_change: Create a Title 5 acting-service rule for commissioned officers, ambassadors, assistant secretaries, inspectors general, service secretaries, U.S. marshals, and deputy directors pending confirmation.
provisions.evidence: Individuals nominated to the following offices will be allowed to act in their capacity whilst waiting confirmation from the United States Senate.
provisions.notes: This is a general appointments-law rule best housed in Title 5.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect upon passage.

current_implementation.detail: The note is too narrow and sits in Title 22, but the rule is broader appointments/vacancies law and should be codified as a real Title 5 provision.

recommended_actions: Remove the Trello link from rp-pl011073-codification.
Codify the acting-service rule as a new Title 5 provision covering all seven enumerated office categories.

laws.title: Public Law 12-74 | Federal Bar Association Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: Section 3 repeals Public Law 7-47 and sections 4-5 establish and govern the Federal Bar Association as a new executive-branch independent establishment. The recovered source contains no self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Federal Bar Association Act of 2023.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and anti-supersession clause.
provisions.evidence: If any provision of this Act, or the application thereof, is held invalid, the validity of the remainder of this Act.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 7-47, the Federal Bar Association Act of 2022.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal of prior public law PL 7-47.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 7-47 | The Federal Bar Association Act of 2022 is hereby repealed.
provisions.notes: Treat as repeal history; do not create permanent Code text for the repeal itself.

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the Federal Bar Association as an independent establishment with chairman, vice chairman, board, bylaws, and seal.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new Title 5 organic provision establishing the Federal Bar Association in the Executive branch as an independent establishment.
provisions.evidence: There is established in the Executive branch an Association to be known as the Federal Bar Association. The Association shall be an independent establishment.
provisions.notes: The external Code of Professional Conduct link should not be embedded as statutory text.

provisions.text_summary: Administrative functions: examination, membership, discipline, law-firm regulation, and sovereign immunity.
provisions.exact_change: Add a Title 5 provision covering the Federal Bar Examination, membership rules, discipline, law-firm regulation, and sovereign immunity.
provisions.evidence: The Federal Bar Examination shall be an exam that is created and administered to citizens by the Chairman.
provisions.notes: Keep the Google Drive link out of the permanent text.

current_implementation.detail: The draft inserted a long boilerplate note at a Title 50 anchor with a full-text dump and Trello link. This is a new Title 5 organic statute, so the current implementation is only a note and is misanchored.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012074-codification.
Codify sections 4-5 as new Title 5 organic provisions for the Federal Bar Association.
Record the repeal of Public Law 7-47 as a historical repeal cross-reference.

laws.title: Public Law 12-75 | Freedom of Information Common Sense Act
laws.status_basis: Section 3 expressly replaces text in 5 U.S.C. section 552, so the act is a live Code amendment. The source contains no self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the Freedom of Information Common Sense Act.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose to lower FOIA response times.
provisions.evidence: It is the purpose of this act to lower the response time limit for all Freedom of Information Act Requests.

provisions.text_summary: Replaces the initial FOIA determination deadline with a 4-day deadline.
provisions.exact_change: Change the FOIA determination deadline in section 552(a)(6)(A)(i) from 20 days to 4 days and require immediate notice.
provisions.evidence: 5 U.S. Code section 552(a)(6)(A)(i) shall be replaced with: determine within 4 days after the receipt of any such request.

provisions.text_summary: Replaces the appeal determination deadline with a 4-day deadline and rewrites the tolling paragraph.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 552(a)(6)(A)(ii) with the quoted 4-day appeal language and the revised commencement paragraph.
provisions.evidence: 5 U.S. Code section 552(a)(6)(A)(ii) shall be replaced with: make a determination with respect to any appeal within four days after the receipt of such appeal.
provisions.notes: The source still says The 20-day period shall not be tolled, which should be flagged for drafting cleanup.

provisions.text_summary: Allows one requester-information request to toll the new 4-day period.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 552(a)(6)(A)(ii)(I) with the one-request tolling language quoted in the source.
provisions.evidence: 5 U.S. Code section 552(a)(6)(A)(ii)(I) shall be replaced with: that the agency may make one request to the requester for information and toll the four-day period.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and immediate effectiveness on full passage.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be severable ... the Freedom of Information Common Sense Act of 2023 will be in effect immediately upon full passage.

current_implementation.detail: The draft note is at a Title 44 anchor with a full-text dump and Trello link, but the real target is 5 U.S.C. section 552. No textual amendment was actually made.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012075-codification.
Amend 5 U.S.C. section 552(a)(6)(A)(i), (ii), and (ii)(I) with the source's replacement language.
Move the codification to Title 5 and flag the residual 20-day-period drafting inconsistency.

provisions.text_summary: No recoverable statutory text is available.
provisions.evidence: source_status unrecoverable; source_reason Access revoked or permission denied; text_extracted false.

Replace the boilerplate with an honest source-limited historical note stating that the source is unrecoverable.

laws.title: Public Law 12-80 | Government Labor Management Act
laws.status_basis: The act creates a comprehensive federal labor-management relations program for government employees and contains no self-repeal in the recovered source.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE. This act shall be referred to as the Government Labor Management Act.

provisions.text_summary: Policy declarations, collective bargaining rights, and supervisor definition.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the labor-management policy statement and employee rights framework in Title 5.
provisions.evidence: The government finds and declares that an effective collective bargaining process is in the general public interest. Each employee of the government has the right.
provisions.notes: Title 5 is the proper home for this federal employee labor statute.

provisions.text_summary: Labor-management relations program administered by DOCAL.
provisions.exact_change: Create the DOCAL labor-management relations program and its core procedures.
provisions.evidence: The Department of Commerce and Labor shall issue rules and regulations establishing a labor-management relations program.

provisions.text_summary: Standards of conduct for labor organizations.
provisions.exact_change: Add labor-organization standards of conduct, certification, and rulemaking authority.
provisions.evidence: Recognition shall be accorded only to a labor organization that is free from corrupt influences.
provisions.notes: The source later reuses section number 4 for unfair labor practices.

provisions.text_summary: Unfair labor practices for the government, employees, labor organizations, agents, and representatives.
provisions.exact_change: Add the unfair labor practice prohibitions and associated enforcement rules.
provisions.evidence: The government, its agents, its departments, and representatives are prohibited from.

provisions.text_summary: Strikes prohibited.
provisions.exact_change: Codify a strike prohibition for government employees and labor organizations.
provisions.evidence: It shall be unlawful for any government employee or labor organization to participate in, authorize, or ratify a strike against the government.

provisions.text_summary: Employee rights and grievance procedure.
provisions.exact_change: Codify employee rights to organize, bargain, and present grievances.
provisions.evidence: All employees shall have the right to organize a labor organization free from interference, restraint, or coercion.

provisions.text_summary: Union security and dues deduction.
provisions.exact_change: Add dues-deduction and service-fee provisions for certified labor organizations.
provisions.evidence: Any labor organization which has been certified as the exclusive representative shall, upon request, have its dues and uniform assessments deducted.

provisions.text_summary: Management rights and matters subject to collective bargaining.
provisions.exact_change: Set out management rights, negotiability rules, and law-enforcement discipline carve-outs.
provisions.evidence: The respective personnel authorities shall retain the sole right, in accordance with applicable laws and rules and regulations.

provisions.text_summary: Unit determination.
provisions.exact_change: Create unit-determination standards for labor organization recognition.
provisions.evidence: The determination of an appropriate unit will be made on a case-to-case basis.

provisions.text_summary: Selection of exclusive representatives and election procedures.
provisions.exact_change: Add recognition, decertification, and election rules for exclusive representatives.
provisions.evidence: Exclusive recognition shall be granted to a labor organization which has been selected by a majority of employees in an appropriate unit.

provisions.text_summary: Rights accompanying exclusive recognition.
provisions.exact_change: Define the rights and duties of the exclusive representative.
provisions.evidence: The labor organization which has been certified to be the exclusive representative of all employees in the unit shall have the right to act for and negotiate agreements.

provisions.text_summary: Sunshine provisions for bargaining and fact-finding proceedings.
provisions.exact_change: Add public-access rules for fact-finding and closed bargaining sessions.
provisions.evidence: Collective bargaining sessions between the government and employee organization representatives shall not be open to the public.

provisions.text_summary: Remedies, enforcement, and judicial review.
provisions.exact_change: Add DOCAL remedial powers and District Court enforcement/review procedures.
provisions.evidence: Remedies of the FEC and DOCAL may include, but shall not be limited to, orders which.

provisions.text_summary: Timelines for DOCAL decisions.
provisions.exact_change: Require DOCAL to render decisions within a reasonable period and no later than two weeks.
provisions.evidence: All decisions of DOCAL shall be rendered within a reasonable period of time, and in no event later than 2 weeks.

provisions.text_summary: Collective bargaining agreements and approvals.
provisions.exact_change: Set approval and submission rules for labor agreements.
provisions.evidence: An agreement with a labor organization is subject to the approval of the President or his or her designee.

provisions.text_summary: Collective bargaining concerning compensation.
provisions.exact_change: Authorize compensation bargaining and associated unit rules.
provisions.evidence: DOCAL shall provide for collective bargaining concerning compensation under the procedures of and on the dates provided in section 17.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effect, severability, and conflict clause.
provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect upon passage of both houses of congress and signed by the president.

current_implementation.detail: The draft note is a long dump at a Title 29 anchor, but this is a federal-employee labor-management statute that belongs in Title 5. No actual new chapter or sections were created, and the source has duplicate section 4 numbering.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012080-codification.
Codify sections 2-16 as a new Title 5 labor-management relations chapter or section set.
Flag the duplicate section 4 numbering for cleanup and keep the short-title and effective-date material as notes.
